text.title: AI Story Truth Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every AI Story Truth Checklist application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Each story names a persona from the catalogue; invented personas rejected.

rationale.source: AI Story Truth Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: AI invents plausible personas that don't exist; catalogue check catches them.

rule.statement: Each story maps to a job from the JTBD catalogue.

rationale.source: AI Story Truth Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: AI conflates jobs ('user wants to be productive'); catalogue check forces specificity.

rule.statement: Story metrics (success / adoption) are cross-checked against an existing source.

rationale.source: AI Story Truth Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: AI invents adoption numbers; cross-check catches them.

rule.statement: Story carries a pointer to ≥2 edge cases (rationale: hallucinations skew happy-path).

rationale.source: AI Story Truth Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: AI drafts skew to happy paths; edge-case pointer forces breadth.

rule.statement: Reviewer is named (handle / role with rotation); no 'BA team'.

rationale.source: AI Story Truth Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Plural review = no review; named reviewer enables attribution.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
